title: BriefSpark Launchpad
preview:
Lead source: inbound from app partner (sales enablement/call-summaries marketplace).

CRM-style summary: brief, pragmatic SMB — 25 employees, US sales org, high call volume (~500 calls/mo across team). Currently uses in-house Node app to stitch Zoom transcriptions; manual redaction and QA steps take ~6-8 hours/wk. G...
text:
Lead source: inbound from app partner (sales enablement/call-summaries marketplace).

CRM-style summary: brief, pragmatic SMB — 25 employees, US sales org, high call volume (~500 calls/mo across team). Currently uses in-house Node app to stitch Zoom transcriptions; manual redaction and QA steps take ~6-8 hours/wk. Goal: reduce manual post-call work; auto-generate 3-5 bullet summaries + action items; flag PII.

PII & retention concerns (customer quotes):
- 'We can't have raw transcripts stored for more than 7 days.'
- 'Need a simple way to redact or avoid storing email/cc numbers in records.'
- 'If we use hosted API, can we ensure transcripts are ephemeral and not used for model training?'

What they care about: latency (wants <1.5s/token for streaming summary), pricing predictability (they run small but frequent jobs), simple SDK and drop-in integration for Node, minimal infra overhea...

text:
Inbound SMB trial. Fast signup; self-serve. Created first key but usage stalled after smoke test. Key facts:
- Primary contact: Thomas Reed (CTO, small engineering team).
- Use-case: searchable product docs + in-app support assistant. Wants embeddings + chat routing as next step.
- Latency target: 200-350ms p95 for short chat flows; throughput low (10-30 qps peak).
- Cost sensitivity: high — looking to minimize per-token spend; asked about batching + prefix caching.
- Model preference: open models OK if cost/latency competitive; asked about Redwood-verified low-cost variants.
- Security: basic SOC2 assurance acceptable for now; will escalate to SSO/SAML if they move to Dedicated.
Challenges/observations:
- After initial SE call, no real requests beyond healthcheck. Likely cause: team started evaluating OpenAI due to aggressive free trial credits and ecosystem plugins.
- Quote from Tho...

title: ClearPoint PayTech
preview: Inbound from seed-stage payments startup. Found us via developer blog + community examples. Started with free credits; test integration looks good. Early security asks: "SOC2 in-scope?" and SSO/SAML support. CTO (Mina Patel) is pragmatic — wants hosted first for speed but asks about VPC/on-prem path if scale/regulat...
text: Inbound from seed-stage payments startup. Found us via developer blog + community examples. Started with free credits; test integration looks good. Early security asks: "SOC2 in-scope?" and SSO/SAML support. CTO (Mina Patel) is pragmatic — wants hosted first for speed but asks about VPC/on-prem path if scale/regulatory requires it. Cost sensitivity: expects bursty chat traffic (~100 reqs/min peak) and heavy embeddings for transaction doc search. Latency target: <150ms p99 for chat; embeddings queries <=50ms desired but flexible. Preference: open models + Redwood's curated model catalog; willing to try Redwood-hosted small LLMs for cost control.

title: SignalMoss CX
preview:
Inbound from product: building a lightweight CX automation layer for small e-commerce merchants. Primary ask = hosted API (self-serve).
- Found us via blog post on structured output + tool-calling. Signed up for free credits 2026-03-01.
- Use-case: automatic ticket summarization + structured JSON output to map to ti...
text:
Inbound from product: building a lightweight CX automation layer for small e-commerce merchants. Primary ask = hosted API (self-serve).
- Found us via blog post on structured output + tool-calling. Signed up for free credits 2026-03-01.
- Use-case: automatic ticket summarization + structured JSON output to map to ticket fields (category, priority, order_id). Also want tool-calling to patch/update tickets in Zendesk and run triage rules.
- Perf targets: p50 summary latency < 300ms for short messages, burst throughput ~20-40 requests/sec during peak; cost sensitive (tight margin for SMB customers).
- Model prefs: prefer Redwood-verified smaller models (13B/quantized) or composite routing: low-cost base for summaries, higher-quality model for escalations. Want auto-fallback to cheaper model when capacity/cost constraints.
- Security: SOC2 required before procurement; need SSO/SAML for ad...

title: Brookline Botworks
preview: Workload type: chat (support assistant) + embeddings for KB search,Latency: p95 < 300ms for short queries; acceptable fallbacks to cheaper model for non-critical queries,Throughput: expected burst up to 30 req/s during peak support hours,Cost sensitivity: medium-high; requests for batching, token limits, and quantiz...
text: Workload type: chat (support assistant) + embeddings for KB search,Latency: p95 < 300ms for short queries; acceptable fallbacks to cheaper model for non-critical queries,Throughput: expected burst up to 30 req/s during peak support hours,Cost sensitivity: medium-high; requests for batching, token limits, and quantized model recommendations,Security: SOC2 required, DPA mandatory, full sub-processor disclosure, SAML SSO, admin audit logs, retention controls,Deployment: hosted API on public cloud; no VPC/on-prem planned in current phase,Model routing/fallback: want automatic fallback to compatible smaller models when cost/capacity thresholds hit,Data handling: need clarity on transient KV cache behavior and whether support transcripts are stored beyond retention window

text:
Lead source: inbound via blog post 'building better RAG for docs'. Small dev-forward SaaS building a searchable knowledge base for SMB customers. Wants hosted API for self-serve POC — low friction.

Key requirements / quotes:
- Workload: embeddings + retriever + reranker pipeline. Primary traffic: user-initiated doc search (web UI) ~100k queries/month.
- Latency target: median query RTT <300ms (embedding + retrieval + reranker).
- Cost sensitivity: high — needs predictable per-query pricing. CTO: "we need predictable per-query cost — we can't absorb wild token spikes".
- Model preferences: open models preferred; interested in Redwood catalog and guidance on quantized variants for cost.
- Vector DB: currently on Supabase; asks for examples / best practice for integrating Redwood hosted API with Supabase and when to use Redis/Weaviate alternatives.
- Reranking: evaluating single-call re...

text: Workload types: customer-support chat + lightweight embeddings for product search,Targets: p95 latency < 300ms for chat (acceptable up to 500ms on peak), throughput ~200 concurrent chat sessions,Cost sensitivity: high — aiming to reduce monthly inference bill by 25-40% without losing answer quality,Model prefs: open model family OK (Llama/Meta variants), willing to test Redwood-verified smaller quantized variants,Routing/fallback: prefer single-region US (NA), want automatic fallback to cheaper model when latency SLOs can still be met,Observability: need token-level cost breakdowns per route and per environment,Optimization asks: batching guidance, prefix/KV caching for chat sessions, prompt compression templates, and a token audit

text: Inbound SMB lead from community blog post. Trial activated with sample support-bot and embedding search. Primary contact: Priya R. (Head of Product). Wants to keep on public cloud / not ready for VPC. Major ask: how to reduce monthly token bill — batching, caching, prompt compression. Quotation: "we love latency now but need predictable cost as we scale to 1k users".

text:
Snapshot from AE/SE interactions and security review. POC never started — fell apart during security and resourcing alignment. Key points:
- 2025-11-04 inbound lead via trade show; initial discovery call (Ava Chen + Diego). Company running camera analytics across 12 facilities, wants near-real-time event classification and operator summarization.
- They insist on private deployment inside their VPC or fully on-prem for 3 'critical' sites. KMS control mandatory; FIPS and export considerations flagged.
- Security sent initial questionnaire (see linked doc). They flagged: no third-party KMS allowed unless HSM integration; 3-year retention + audit export requirements.
- SE calls 2025-12-03 demo + architecture walkthrough. Ironwood liked runtime performance claims but asked for proof of KMS/HSM integration and proof of audit log retention.
- Procurement then announced a company-wide cap on...

text:
Inbound self-serve sign-up via hosted API trial. SMB web agency building marketing assistants that call internal tools (pricing lookup, ticket creation). 

Lead source and context:
- signed up with company email 2025-11-02, used free credits to run quick prototypes
- submitted support thread reporting streaming / function-calling issues 2025-11-06

Summary of current problem (customer reporting):
- Using streaming responses with structured JSON mode + functions (tool-calling). Expected: continuous chunked JSON building then final function call. Actual: intermittent malformed JSON chunks causing client-side JSON.parse failures.
- Example error reported: "JSONParseError: Unexpected token '}' in stream chunk" and "InvalidFunctionArgs: parameter 'price' expected number but got string".
- Customer paraphrase in call: "The stream looks right in the console but our frontend chokes when a chu...

Procurement message 2/22 indicated a company-wide freeze on external vendors until May; phrased as "pausing vendor projects" — unclear if permanent. No internal champion surfaced after initial call; Head of ML Ops reading messages but not committing to next steps. text:
Intro / qualification summary:
- Initial inbound via form (Mar 2). Identified as platform/ops team evaluating LLM to power customer chat + doc search across vessel ops docs.
- Quick qual call w/ Ethan Park + Priya N. (Mar 8). Attendees: Head of Platform (Sara Miles), Senior Infra Eng (Tom Ruiz).
- Key metrics: target p95 latency <= 250ms for short chat queries; burst throughput ~400 qps during peak; sensitive PII redaction requirements.
- Interested in hosted API for PoC to move fast, but long-term preference is VPC/private deployment to meet data residency and KMS controls.

Technical and security notes:
- Security team requires SOC2 + encryption-at-rest via customer KMS; data residency requirement: EU for parts of customer data (they operate NA/EU fleets).
- Concerned about KV-cache persistence and logs containing manifest data. Asked about retention controls and audit logs.
- SE ac...
